name: hummus
instructions: Throw everything in a blender cold and blend until smooth.
notes:
HUMMUS vs ROMESCO: hummus and romesco share some ingredient surface area — peppers, almonds, and pine nuts are reasonably interchangeable between the two — but the two dishes have opposite acid preferences and opposite centers of gravity.

- ROMESCO has a soft preference for red wine vinegar and strongly dislikes lemon. The acid axis lives on the vinegar side. The center of gravity is the pepper-and-tomato base; nuts and bread are doing thickening / textural work.
- HUMMUS needs lemon to be edible. The lemon-and-tahini axis is what makes hummus taste like itself; vinegar is wrong. The center of gravity is the beans — chickpeas (or white beans) are the dish, not a thickener.

Don't add vinegar to hummus in lieu of lemon juice. Different acid, different cuisine register, different result.

BEAN CHOICE: chickpeas are canonical. White beans work alongside or as a partial substitute — they make the texture creamier and slightly milder.

NUT / SEED ADDITIONS: pine nuts and almonds add body and a richer mouthfeel. Both can ride in the blender with the chickpeas.

BELL PEPPERS: optional roasted-pepper addition for color and sweetness. Pushes the dish toward a red-hummus / romesco-adjacent register without committing to romesco's vinegar axis.
